activities.name: Celebrating Shaun
activities.description: My nephew Shaun Champion has been swimming competitively most of his life, and yesterday won the 50m butterfly in the Australian National Swimming Championship. My daily run habit is nothing compared to the training discipline it has taken him to get to these heights at the ripe old age of 21. I salute him. Onwards Shaun!

Today is my last day on the job for Microsoft. I am retiring from paid work, and will be making myself useful in other ways.

Coming soon: David’s Rules of Retirement Running. Stay tuned.

activities.name: Testing the course for “FOD’s Frontyard Ultra”
activities.description:
FOD stands for Friends of David, which is everyone who reads this message. Each lap starts and ends in front of my house, so I claim Green Lake and Woodland Park for my front yard! Google “Bigs Backyard Ultra” if you aren’t familiar with events like this.

When will it happen? I’m thinking a weekend in Spring, but not concrete plans yet. 

Leave a comment if you’re even slightly curious about this, and I’ll keep you in the loop as planning progresses.

activities.name: Hills and canyon with Mintu and Anita
Proposed date: May 28-29, 2022 for David's Front Yard Ultra
activities.description:
Those of you who are even mildly interested in participating in my little endurance event for runners (and walkers see below), please let me know if Memorial Day weekend is problematic. It's not locked in stone yet.

Here's how it works: The gun goes off at noon on Saturday, and goes off again on the hour every hour until nobody is left. During each hour, the runners must complete the 4.167 mile circuit shown in this run. The walkers must complete the 2.8 mile circuit of the inner Green Lake trail.

The catch is that you may not start another lap until the top of the hour so there is no point running fast. In fact, the people who go the longest in events like this generally shuffle along at a 12 minute pace giving themselves a 10 minute break to sit down, eat, rest, and question their sanity before fronting up to the start line gain.  Walkers who maintain 3.3 MPH will get a similar break.

There will be prizes for 3 laps, 6 laps, 12 laps, and even 24 laps if anyone is crazy enough to keep going that long. (The distance is chosen so that anyone who can last 24 hours will have run 100 miles, or walked 100 km)

This is a free, self-supported, informal event that will not be publicly advertised. Like this post of you are interested, and feel free to reach out to me on email if you have questions. (david @ gedye  .  com).
